memoryArtifacts.headline: Archambaud de Dampierre, seigneur de Bourbon et de Montluçon at the Battle of Taillebourg
memoryArtifacts.description: The battle of Taillebourg: King Henry III advanced to Tonnay-Charente by mid-July and Louis moved to Saint-Jean-d'Angély, just north of Taillebourg, the armies intending to reach the bridge across the Charente River, located in the commune of Taillebourg. Henry and Hugh positioned their army near the village of Saint-James on the west bank of the river and camped in the neighbouring field, while Louis was welcomed to the fortified chateau of Geoffroy de Rancon, the Count of Taillebourg. Henry decided to send an advance guard to protect the left bank of the Taillebourg bridge, a move that led to a sharp encounter with some French troops on either July 21 or 22. Louis decided to follow up this engagement and launched a full offensive with the entire French army. The aggressive French assaults carried the day and the English king fled south to the town of Saintes, along with the revolting barons.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Archambaud VIII de Bourbon於1189年12月17日出生在法國奧維阿利也布爾波-利阿哈波奧爾特時，他的父親Guy II de Dampierre24歲，母親Mathilde Ire de Bourbon24歲。他於1205年與Alix Guigone de Forez在法國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1242年7月23日在法國努維勒-阿吉泰哈倫-馬利塔爾波格享年52歲，埋葬在法國奧維普-德-o維萊特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Archambaud VIII de Bourbon于1189年12月17日出生在法国奧維阿利也布爾波-利阿哈波奧爾特时，他的父亲Guy II de Dampierre24岁，母亲Mathilde Ire de Bourbon24岁。他于1205年与Alix Guigone de Forez在法国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1242年7月23日在法国努維勒-阿吉泰哈倫-馬利塔爾波格享年52岁，埋葬在法国奧維普-德-o維萊特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourbon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: VIII
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine pet form of   Vivien   and   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Archambaud VIII de Bourbon was born on 17 December 1189, in Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, France, his father, Guy II de Dampierre, was 24 and his mother, Mathilde Ire de Bourbon, was 24. He married Alix Guigone de Forez in 1205, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 23 July 1242, in Taillebourg, Charente-Maritime, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, at the age of 52, and was buried in Virlet, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Archambaud VIII de Bourbon est né le 17 décembre 1189, à Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, France, son père, Guy II de Dampierre, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Mathilde Ire de Bourbon, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Alix Guigone de Forez en 1205, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 23 juillet 1242, à Taillebourg, Charente-Maritime, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Virlet, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, France.

Dezember 1189 in Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy II de Dampierre 24 und seine Mutter Mathilde Ire de Bourbon 24. Er hat 1205 in Frankreich Alix Guigone de Forez geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Juli 1242 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Taillebourg, Charente-Maritime, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich und wurde in Virlet, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Frankreich begraben.

Lui sposato Alix Guigone de Forez nel 1205, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 23 luglio 1242, nel Taillebourg, Charente-Maritime, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Virlet, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Archambaud VIII de Bourbonが1189年12月17日にフランスアウバーグンアリールボウルボン-ル'アルチャンバウルトで生まれた時，父Guy II de Dampierreは24歳，母Mathilde Ire de Bourbonは24歳であった。1205年にフランスでAlix Guigone de Forezと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1242年7月23日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインチャーエンテ-マリティメティイレボウルグで52歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグンプウイ-デ-ドoメバーレットにて埋葬された。

ko.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 폰테브라트-'압베이
ko.lifeSummary: Archambaud VIII de Bourbon(이)가 1189년 12월 17일 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어 부르보-'아르챔바트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy II de Dampierre(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde Ire de Bourbon(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1205년 프랑스에서 Alix Guigone de Forez(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1242년 7월 23일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 처테-마이팀 테이레보우르그에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니 푸이-데-드o 버렛에 묻혔다.

pt.17 December 1189–23 July 1242: 17 de dezembro de 1189–23 de julho de 1242
pt.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Archambaud VIII de Bourbon nasceu em 17 de dezembro de 1189, em Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, França, seu pai, Guy II de Dampierre, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde Ire de Bourbon, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Alix Guigone de Forez em 1205, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de julho de 1242, em Taillebourg, Charente-Maritime, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Virlet, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, França.

ro.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Franţa

ru.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Archambaud VIII de Bourbon родился 17 декабря 1189 г. в местности бурбон-л'арчамбаулт, аллиир, аувиргн, Франция, его отцу по имени Guy II de Dampierre было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Mathilde Ire de Bourbon, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Alix Guigone de Forez в 1205 г. в местности Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 23 июля 1242 г. в местности теиллебург, чаренте-маритим, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности вирлет, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргн, Франция.

es.17 December 1189–23 July 1242: 17 de diciembre de 1189–23 de julio de 1242
es.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Archambaud VIII de Bourbon nació el 17 de diciembre de 1189, en Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, su papá, Guy II de Dampierre, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Mathilde Ire de Bourbon, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Alix Guigone de Forez en 1205, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de julio de 1242, en Taillebourg, Charente-Maritime, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Virlet, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne, Francia.
